steps.title: Extract the given values
steps.content: From the figure, AC = 32 and angle ABC = 90°. From the problem, AB = AC − 11 = 32 − 11 = 21.

steps.title: Apply the area equality
steps.content:
The area of triangle ABC can be computed two ways: using the two legs, or using the hypotenuse and the altitude to it.

Area = (1/2) · AB · BC
Area = (1/2) · AC · BD

Setting these equal and simplifying:
$$AB \cdot BC = AC \cdot BD$$

steps.title: Solve for the ratio
steps.content:
Divide both sides by BD · AB:
$$\frac{BC}{BD} = \frac{AC}{AB} = \frac{32}{21}$$

This equals approximately 1.524.
